portal.heading: Welcome to {{name}}
portal.subtitle: Browse available services below.
portal.emptyHint: There are no public services running at the moment.

login.description:
Deploy containers. Manage datasets.
Monitor your data warehouse. All in one place.
login.cliAuthPrompt: Select a login method to authenticate muveectl

projects.heading: All Projects
projects.noProjectsHint: Create your first project to get started
projects.skillUrlDesc: Share this URL with Claude to teach it how to use the muveectl CLI on your behalf.
projects.installCmdDesc: Run this in your terminal to install muveectl and set up Claude Code skill support.

projectDetail.tunnelHint: This is a tunnel project. Start the tunnel with: muveectl tunnel --project {{name}} <PORT>
projectDetail.pushUrlHint: Use any username and your API token as the password.

repo.empty: Repository is empty. Push some code to get started.

projectDetail.noDeployments: No deployments yet. Click Deploy to start.

config.iconPlaceholder: Paste SVG code or enter an image URL
config.iconHint: Paste inline SVG code or an image URL (https://...). Used as the app icon in the service portal.
config.memoryLimitHint: Container memory cap, leave empty for no limit. E.g. 512m, 2g, 8g
config.volumeMountPathHint: Container-internal mount path (e.g. /workspace). Leave empty to disable persistent storage. Requires VOLUME_NFS_BASE_PATH on the server.
config.authDocsHint: Learn how your app can use muvee auth (userinfo, logout, CLI access)
config.domainOptional: Optional — defaults to "{{name}}" if left blank
config.domainRequired: Required — project name cannot be used as a subdomain

datasets.hint: Select datasets to mount into the container. <accent>dependency</accent> = rsync copy (LRU cached). <blue>readwrite</blue> = direct NFS mount.
datasets.empty: No datasets available. Create one in the Datasets section.

workspace.noVolume: No persistent storage path configured. Set a volume mount path in the Config tab and redeploy.
workspace.notConfigured: VOLUME_NFS_BASE_PATH is not configured on the server. Contact your administrator.
workspace.name: Name

secrets.hint: Attach secrets for runtime env vars, git clone auth, or docker build secrets.
secrets.empty: No secrets found. Create secrets in the
secrets.buildSecretHint: Used as /run/secrets/<id> in Dockerfile RUN steps.

tokens.hint: Project-scoped API tokens for git push and CLI access. Each token only works with this project.
tokens.created: Token created — copy it now, you won't see it again.
tokens.copyWarning: This token will not be displayed again. Store it securely.
tokens.deleteConfirm: Delete this token? Any git remotes or scripts using it will stop working.
tokens.empty: No tokens yet. Create one to enable git push or CLI access for this project.

datasets.heading: Datasets
datasets.emptyHint: Register a dataset sub-path to start tracking

newProject.heading: Create Project

fields.name: Name
fields.descriptionPlaceholder: A short description of this service
fields.tagsHint: Comma-separated tags for categorizing the service.
fields.iconPlaceholder: Paste SVG code or enter an image URL
fields.iconHint: Paste inline SVG code or an image URL. Used as the app icon in the service portal.
fields.memoryLimitHint: Container memory cap, leave empty for no limit. E.g. 512m, 2g, 8g
fields.volumeMountPathHint: Container-internal mount path (e.g. /workspace). Leave empty to disable.

projectType.tunnelHint: Reserve a domain prefix for tunnel access. No git repo or container — traffic is forwarded from your local machine via muveectl tunnel.

gitSource.hostedHint: A git repository will be created for you. After project creation, you'll get a push URL.

hostedCreated.heading: Repository Created
hostedCreated.description: Your hosted repository for "{{name}}" is ready. Push your code using the URL below.
hostedCreated.authHint: Use any username and your Muvee API token as the password when prompted for credentials.

newProject.domainOptional: Optional — defaults to "{{name}}" if left blank
newProject.domainRequired: Required — project name cannot be used as a subdomain
newProject.domainTunnelHint: Required — this prefix will be your tunnel's subdomain

privateRepo.noCredentials: No credentials — repository must be publicly accessible.
privateRepo.githubPatHint: Generate a fine-grained PAT at GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → Fine-grained tokens (Contents: Read-only).
privateRepo.genericPatHint: Enter a Personal Access Token with read access to the repository.
privateRepo.genericUsernameHint: Usually your account username or a fixed value like "x-access-token"
privateRepo.newPatSecretHint: A new secret named "{{name}} Git Token" will be created and bound to this project.
privateRepo.privateKeyHint: Paste the private key (PEM format). Add the corresponding public key as a Deploy Key in your repository settings.
privateRepo.newSshSecretHint: A new secret named "{{name}} Deploy Key" will be created and bound to this project.

errors.noToken: Please enter the token value, or choose "None" if the repository is public.
errors.noSshKey: Please paste the private key, or choose "None" if the repository is public.

secrets.heading: Secrets
secrets.infoText: Secrets are encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM). Values are write-only — they cannot be retrieved after creation. Attach secrets to projects from the project's Secrets tab.
secrets.deleteConfirm: Delete this secret? Projects using it will lose access on next deploy.

form.title: New Secret
form.name: NAME
form.sshKeyHint: Paste the private key (PEM format). You can enable it as the git clone key in the project's Secrets tab.

empty.title: No Secrets Yet
empty.hint: Add passwords, API tokens, or SSH keys to securely inject into your deployments.

secrets.disabledBanner: Secrets are disabled. Set SECRET_ENCRYPTION_KEY in your .env file and restart the server to enable this feature.

newDataset.heading: New Dataset
newDataset.name: NAME
newDataset.nfsPathHint: Relative dataset sub-path under DATASET_NFS_BASE_PATH (e.g. warehouse)

nodes.heading: Nodes
nodes.empty: No nodes registered yet. Start an Agent to connect nodes.
nodes.noBuilder: No builder node registered. Builds will not work until a builder agent is connected.
nodes.noDeployer: No deploy node registered. Deployments will not work until a deploy agent is connected.

tunnels.heading: Tunnels
tunnels.noActive: No active tunnels. Use muveectl tunnel <PORT> to create one.

authorization.pendingBanner: Your request to publish services is pending approval.
authorization.requiredBanner: You need authorization to publish services.

0.title: Welcome to your Private Cloud
0.desc: Let's get your platform set up. Start by customising the branding so it feels like yours.

1.title: System Health Check
1.desc: Running diagnostics to make sure all infrastructure components are working correctly.

2.title: Connect Agents
2.desc: At least one builder and one deployer agent are required. Follow the instructions below to start them.

3.title: You're all set!
3.desc: Your platform is ready. Head to the dashboard to create your first project.

health.hasErrors: Some checks failed. You can still continue, but some features may not work correctly.

done.heading: Setup Complete
done.desc: Your platform is ready. You can adjust branding and settings any time from the admin panel.
done.feat1: Deploy containerised apps from any Git repository
done.feat2: Manage datasets on NFS with version tracking
done.feat3: Monitor your infrastructure in real time

branding.title: Branding
branding.logoHint: Displayed in the sidebar. Use a square image for best results.

authorization.title: Authorization
authorization.description: Control whether users need admin approval before they can publish services. When disabled, all authenticated users can publish freely.
authorization.enabledHint: New users will only be able to browse services until an admin approves their publishing request.

certs.title: HTTPS Certificates
certs.hint: ACME state for every domain muvee expects Traefik to serve. A deploy is only fully ready once its certificate is issued — before that, browsers see the default self-signed cert.
certs.empty: No domains configured — set BASE_DOMAIN in .env to start managing certificates.
certs.storeError: Cannot read {{path}}. Make sure the letsencrypt volume is mounted read-only into muvee-server.
